event_log.description: Event log backend selection. Optional — absent defaults to in-memory.

hooks.description: Hook configuration. Empty vec means no hooks.

max_pre_response_retries.description: Maximum number of LLM regeneration attempts when a PreResponse hook blocks. Default: 2. Max: 5.

memory.description: Memory store configuration. Optional -- absent means no memory stores.

request_end_concurrency.description: Maximum concurrent RequestEnd hook tasks. Excess tasks are dropped with a warning. Default: 64.

router.description: Router configuration — the single source of truth for model routing and classification. Replaces the old `[llm]` and `[classifier]` sections.

max_commands.description: Maximum number of commands to include regardless of threshold.

model_path.description: Path to the ONNX model file.

threshold.description: Relevance score threshold. Commands scoring below this are excluded.

tokenizer_path.description: Path to the tokenizer JSON file.

enabled.description: Whether this domain is enabled. Default: true.

threshold.description: Score threshold for this domain. For the Commands domain: overrides `classifier.threshold` for filtering. For the Model domain: acts as a confidence gate -- if no model scores above this threshold, the default model is used instead. If absent, always pick the highest-scoring model. For ToolNecessity/Memory: overrides classifier.threshold.

memory.description: Memory routing domain config (interface only -- no memory stores yet).

EventLogConfig.description:
Event log backend configuration.

Controls which `EventLog` implementation the binary constructs at startup. Absent or `backend = "memory"` uses `InMemoryEventLog` (no persistence). `backend = "postgres"` requires `database_url` and uses `PostgresEventLog`.

database_url.description: PostgreSQL connection URL. Required when `backend = "postgres"`. Supports `env:VAR_NAME` prefix for loading from the environment.

max_command_iterations.description: Maximum iterations of the command loop before giving up.

request_timeout_secs.description: Overall request timeout in seconds. Covers the entire gateway loop including all LLM calls and command executions. Default: 300 (5 minutes).

system_prompt.description: System prompt prepended to every conversation.

agent.description: Agent name for Weft hook. Required when `hook_type` is `weft`.

event.description: Which lifecycle event this hook fires on.

matcher.description: Optional regex matcher. Absence means "fire on all events of this type."

priority.description: Execution priority. Lower values run first. Default: 100.

script.description: Path to Rhai script file. Required when `hook_type` is `rhai`.

secret.description: Optional shared secret for HTTP hook authentication. Sent as `Authorization: Bearer <secret>` header. Supports `env:VAR_NAME` syntax for environment variable resolution. Only applicable to HTTP hooks; ignored for other types.

timeout_ms.description: Execution timeout in milliseconds. Default: 100 for Rhai, 5000 for HTTP. Max: 5000 for Rhai, 30000 for HTTP.

url.description: URL for HTTP webhook. Required when `hook_type` is `http`.

HookEvent.description:
Lifecycle events that hooks can attach to.

Lives in `weft_core::config` so `HookConfig` can reference it for deserialization. Placing it in the `weft` binary crate would create a circular dependency.

oneOf.description: Request arrives at gateway, before any processing.
Before each routing decision (fires per domain).
After each routing decision (fires per domain).
Before executing a single command invocation.
After command returns, before result injection. Cannot block.
Before building the final HTTP response.
After response sent to client. Cannot block. Fire-and-forget.

HookType.description: Hook type: which execution engine handles this hook.

MemoryConfig.description: Memory configuration. Optional -- absent means no memory stores.

capabilities.description: What this store can do: `read`, `write`, or both. Determines whether the store is eligible for `/recall` (read) and `/remember` (write) operations. Defaults to `["read", "write"]` if omitted.

examples.description: Example queries that this store is best suited for. Used by the semantic router to build centroid embeddings for the Memory domain. Must contain at least one example.

max_results.description: Maximum number of memories to retrieve per query.

name.description: Unique name for this store (e.g., "conversations", "code_knowledge"). Used as the routing candidate ID in the Memory domain.

request_timeout_ms.description:
Request timeout in milliseconds.

**Deferred (v1):** This field is parsed and stored but NOT enforced per-request on the gRPC client. The overall gateway request timeout (`gateway.request_timeout_secs`) is the safety net. The field exists for forward compatibility: when per-store timeouts are implemented (via tonic's `timeout()` on the channel or per-request deadline), existing configs will already have the value.

ModelEntry.description:
A model available for routing, nested under a provider.

The model inherits the provider's API endpoint (wire_format, api_key, base_url). The `model` field is sent to the provider API to select which model to use.

capabilities.description: Capabilities this model supports. Default: `["chat_completions"]` -- a model that doesn't declare capabilities is assumed to support chat completions only.

examples.description: Example queries that this model is best suited for. The router embeds all examples, averages them into a centroid vector, and routes based on distance to the centroid. Must contain at least one example.

model.description: Model identifier sent to the provider API. e.g., "claude-sonnet-4-20250514", "gpt-4o", "llama3.2"

name.description: Unique routing name for this model (e.g., "complex", "fast"). Used as the routing candidate ID. Must be globally unique across all providers.

ProviderConfig.description: An LLM provider -- an API endpoint that serves one or more models.

api_key.description: API key for this provider. Supports `env:VAR_NAME` prefix. For local providers (e.g., Ollama) that don't require authentication, use a literal string like `"unused"`. The value is passed through to the provider's HTTP client (e.g., `Authorization: Bearer unused`). Local providers typically ignore this header, so any non-empty string is acceptable. The field is required (not Optional) to keep the config schema simple.

base_url.description: Optional base URL override. Required for local/custom endpoints (e.g., Ollama).

models.description: Models served by this provider. Must have at least one.

wire_format.description: Wire format -- determines the API protocol used.

wire_script.description: Path to Rhai wire format script. Required when `wire_format` is `custom`. Relative paths are resolved from the current working directory at startup.

RouterConfig.description: Router configuration — the single source of truth for model routing and classification.

classifier.description: Classifier (ONNX bi-encoder) configuration. Previously a top-level [classifier] section.

default_model.description: Default model name. Must reference a model name from one of the providers. If absent, the first model of the first provider is the default.

providers.description: Provider definitions. Each provider is an API endpoint with one or more models. Defaults to empty — validation enforces at least one provider.

skip_tools_when_unnecessary.description: Whether to skip command injection when the router determines tools aren't needed. Default: true.

StoreCapability.description: What a memory store can do. Config-level declaration by the admin -- not part of the gRPC contract.

WireFormat.description: Wire format -- determines how requests are serialized and responses are parsed for a provider's API.

oneOf.description: OpenAI Chat Completions API format. Used by OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, Ollama, vLLM, and most OpenAI-compatible providers.
Custom wire format defined by a Rhai script.
